title: dracaena newtoniana
intro: Dracaena newtoniana, also known as the dragon tree or dragon's blood tree, is a plant species within the genus Dracaena. The plant's resin, which is often referred to as "dragon's blood," has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Dracaena newtoniana have also been associated with various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiviral, and antimicrobial effects, which can help protect against diseases and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the resin is often used in tinctures, salves, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, while also being used as a natural dye in various industries.
